provenance.source_corpus: Establishment of a Vegetation Cover on Tundra Kimberlite Mine Tailings: 2. A Field Study

nodes.name: addition of structure-improving and nutrient-providing amendments

source_spans.text: With the addition of structure-improving and nutrient-providing amendments, plant growth on these kimberlite tailings under field conditions was significantly improved over unamended tailings material.

With the addition of structure-improving and nutrient-providing amendments, plant growth on these kimberlite tailings under field conditions was significantly improved over unamended tailings material.
Tailings properties, including cation exchange capacity, organic carbon, and macronutrient availability, were also improved with amendment addition.
Tailings properties, including cation exchange capacity, organic carbon, and macronutrient availability, were also improved with amendment addition.
Tailings properties, including cation exchange capacity, organic carbon, and macronutrient availability, were also improved with amendment addition.
CEC was significantly increased by peat moss at 10–20 cm
Tailings properties, including cation exchange capacity, organic carbon, and macronutrient availability, were also improved with amendment addition.
Tailings properties, including cation exchange capacity, organic carbon, and macronutrient availability, were also improved with amendment addition.
Sewage sludge significantly increased available nitrate at 10- to 20- and 20- to 30-cm depths
Sewage sludge significantly increased available nitrate at 10- to 20- and 20- to 30-cm depths
P availability was significantly increased with sewage sludge
Peat moss significantly increased P availability at 0–10 cm
CEC was significantly decreased by lake sediment at 10–20 cm
ground cover (32%) significantly increased with sewage sludge
Sewage sludge, peat moss, and lake sediment in combination significantly increased plant height
Sewage sludge, peat moss, or lake sediment significantly increased shoot biomass
Peat moss performed well, increasing water- and nutrient-holding capacities
Peat moss performed well, increasing water- and nutrient-holding capacities
Peat moss performed well, increasing water- and nutrient-holding capacities and overall tailings structure.
